basic_details.job_title: ESIC Teaching Faculty Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Offline for 118 Posts
basic_details.short_information: ESIC (Employees' State Insurance Corporation) is recruiting 118 Professors across 22 medical specialties in ESIC PGIMSRs and ESIC Medical Colleges on a direct recruitment basis. The post carries Pay Level-13 (₹1,23,100-₹2,15,900) plus Non-Practising Allowance. The last date to apply is 27 July 2026, and selection will be conducted through an interview by a Selection Board. Applications must be sent by post to the Regional Office, Delhi.

application_fee.details: Candidates wondering about the fee structure can note the following category-wise breakdown:

age_limit.details: Maximum age: 50 years as on the closing date (27 July 2026) SC/ST candidates: 5 years relaxation (upper limit: 55 years) OBC candidates: 3 years relaxation (upper limit: 53 years) PwBD candidates: 10 years relaxation (upper limit: 60 years; SC/ST PwBD: 15 years, OBC PwBD: 13 years) - subject to a maximum of 56 years ESIC/Government employees: 5 years relaxation Ex-Servicemen: 5 years relaxation, subject to minimum 6 months service after attestation

qualification.details: Understanding who qualifies is essential before applying. The eligibility conditions differ slightly for medical and non-medical candidates, and both must meet specific experience thresholds recognized by the National Medical Commission (NMC). | Maximum age: 50 years as on the closing date (27 July 2026) SC/ST candidates: 5 years relaxation (upper limit: 55 years) OBC candidates: 3 years relaxation (upper limit: 53 years) PwBD candidates: 10 years relaxation (upper limit: 60 years; SC/ST PwBD: 15 years, OBC PwBD: 13 years) - subject to a maximum of 56 years ESIC/Government employees: 5 years relaxation Ex-Servicemen: 5 years relaxation, subject to minimum 6 months service after attestation | For Medical Candidates (Essential): | A recognized medical qualification included in the First or Second Schedule or Part-II of the Third Schedule to the Indian Medical Council Act, 1956. A postgraduate qualification - MD (Doctor of Medicine) or MS (Master of Surgery) or a recognized equivalent in the respective subject or allied discipline. | For Non-Medical Candidates (Essential): | A Master's Degree in the concerned subject or allied discipline. A Doctorate degree from a recognized university in the respective subject or allied discipline. | Candidates must fulfill one of the following: | Option A: Four years of teaching experience as a Reader or Associate Professor in a recognized medical college in the concerned specialty. | Option B: Three years of teaching experience as an Associate Professor in a recognized medical college, plus a minimum of four research publications in indexed or national journals - published as the First Author in journals of National Associations or Societies of the respective specialties. Of these, at least two publications must have been during the tenure as Associate Professor.

salary_details.details: The Professor post carries Pay Level-13 in the Central Government Pay Matrix, with a pay range of ₹1,23,100 to ₹2,15,900 per month. In addition to basic pay, selected candidates are entitled to: | Dearness Allowance (DA) Non-Practising Allowance (NPA) - as admissible to analogous posts in the Central Health Service House Rent Allowance (HRA) Transport Allowance | Promotional avenues are available under the Dynamic Assured Career Progression (DACP) guidelines of the Government of India. | Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

selection_process: The selection for ESIC Professor 2026 is based entirely on an interview conducted by a Selection Board. There is no written examination. Candidates who have applied will be assessed on experience and merit before being called.
Application Submission - Send the completed application form by Speed Post to reach ESIC Regional Office
Delhi by 27 July 2026. Shortlisting - If the number of applications is large
ESIC will shortlist candidates based on teaching experience at Associate Professor/Reader level or higher

beyond the minimum eligibility. Shortlisting is based solely on information and certificates submitted with the application - no subsequent documents will be accepted. Interview - Shortlisted candidates appear before the Selection Board at a venue decided by ESIC. Minimum Qualifying Marks in Interview (out of 100): UR / EWS: 50 marks OBC: 45 marks SC / ST / PwBD: 40 marks Tie-Breaking (if scores are equal): First: Elder candidate preferred. Second: Candidate who passed the essential postgraduate degree earlier. Third: Candidate who completed internship earlier. Reserve List - ESIC may draw a Reserve List as per UPSC guidelines. Probation - Selected candidates will be appointed on probation as per rules.
how_to_apply: Candidates cannot apply online for this recruitment. The application is purely offline via Speed Post.
Download the application form from the official ESIC notification (available at www.esic.gov.in).
Fill in the application form completely in block letters. Ensure at least 10 state preferences for posting are listed.
Attach a recent self-attested passport-size photograph firmly (not stapled) on the application form.
Prepare a Demand Draft/Banker's Cheque of ₹500 (where applicable) in favour of 'ESI Fund Account No. 2', payable at Delhi. Write your name and address on the reverse side.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents (see Documents Required section below).
Place the DD/BC pinned or clipped (not stapled or tagged) at the top of the application.
Superscribe the envelope: "Application for the post of Professor for Medical Institutions"
Send by Speed Post to: Regional Office Delhi, ESI Corporation DDA Complex Cum Office, 3rd & 4th Floor,, Rajendra Bhawan, Rajendra Place,, New Delhi - 110008
Ensure delivery by 27 July 2026. ESIC will not be responsible for postal delays.

content_sections.heading: Vacancy Breakdown: 118 Posts Across 22 Specialties
content_sections.text: The 118 Professor vacancies span clinical and pre-clinical disciplines. Reserved categories include backlog vacancies of 11 for SC, 6 for ST, and 23 for OBC. Eight posts are additionally reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD).

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria for ESIC Professor 2026
content_sections.text: Understanding who qualifies is essential before applying. The eligibility conditions differ slightly for medical and non-medical candidates, and both must meet specific experience thresholds recognized by the National Medical Commission (NMC). Maximum age: 50 years as on the closing date (27 July 2026) SC/ST candidates: 5 years relaxation (upper limit: 55 years) OBC candidates: 3 years relaxation (upper limit: 53 years) PwBD candidates: 10 years relaxation (upper limit: 60 years; SC/ST PwBD: 15 years, OBC PwBD: 13 years) - subject to a maximum of 56 years ESIC/Government employees: 5 years relaxation Ex-Servicemen: 5 years relaxation, subject to minimum 6 months service after attestation For Medical Candidates (Essential): A recognized medical qualification included in the First or Second Schedule or Part-II of the Third Schedule to the Indian Medical Council Act, 1956. A postgraduate qualification - MD (Doctor of Medicine) or MS (Master of Surgery) or a recognized equivalent in the respective subject or allied discipline. For Non-Medical Candidates (Essential): A Master's Degree in the concerned subject or allied discipline. A Doctorate degree from a recognized university in the respective subject or allied discipline. Candidates must fulfill one of the following: Option A: Four years of teaching experience as a Reader or Associate Professor in a recognized medical college in the concerned specialty. Option B: Three years of teaching experience as an Associate Professor in a recognized medical college, plus a minimum of four research publications in indexed or national journals - published as the First Author in journals of National Associations or Societies of the respective specialties. Of these, at least two publications must have been during the tenure as Associate Professor.

content_sections.heading: ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about the fee structure can note the following category-wise breakdown:

content_sections.heading: ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 - Pay Scale and Benefits
content_sections.text: The Professor post carries Pay Level-13 in the Central Government Pay Matrix, with a pay range of ₹1,23,100 to ₹2,15,900 per month. In addition to basic pay, selected candidates are entitled to: Dearness Allowance (DA) Non-Practising Allowance (NPA) - as admissible to analogous posts in the Central Health Service House Rent Allowance (HRA) Transport Allowance Promotional avenues are available under the Dynamic Assured Career Progression (DACP) guidelines of the Government of India. Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

content_sections.heading: ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process
content_sections.text: The selection for ESIC Professor 2026 is based entirely on an interview conducted by a Selection Board. There is no written examination. Candidates who have applied will be assessed on experience and merit before being called. Application Submission - Send the completed application form by Speed Post to reach ESIC Regional Office, Delhi by 27 July 2026. Shortlisting - If the number of applications is large, ESIC will shortlist candidates based on teaching experience at Associate Professor/Reader level or higher, beyond the minimum eligibility. Shortlisting is based solely on information and certificates submitted with the application - no subsequent documents will be accepted. Interview - Shortlisted candidates appear before the Selection Board at a venue decided by ESIC. Minimum Qualifying Marks in Interview (out of 100): UR / EWS: 50 marks OBC: 45 marks SC / ST / PwBD: 40 marks Tie-Breaking (if scores are equal): First: Elder candidate preferred. Second: Candidate who passed the essential postgraduate degree earlier. Third: Candidate who completed internship earlier. Reserve List - ESIC may draw a Reserve List as per UPSC guidelines. Probation - Selected candidates will be appointed on probation as per rules.

content_sections.heading: ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 - How to Apply Step-by-Step
content_sections.text: Candidates cannot apply online for this recruitment. The application is purely offline via Speed Post. Download the application form from the official ESIC notification (available at www.esic.gov.in ). Fill in the application form completely in block letters. Ensure at least 10 state preferences for posting are listed. Attach a recent self-attested passport-size photograph firmly (not stapled) on the application form. Prepare a Demand Draft/Banker's Cheque of ₹500 (where applicable) in favour of 'ESI Fund Account No. 2', payable at Delhi. Write your name and address on the reverse side. Attach self-attested copies of all required documents (see Documents Required section below). Place the DD/BC pinned or clipped (not stapled or tagged) at the top of the application. Superscribe the envelope: "Application for the post of Professor for Medical Institutions" Send by Speed Post to: Regional Office Delhi, ESI Corporation DDA Complex Cum Office, 3rd & 4th Floor,, Rajendra Bhawan, Rajendra Place,, New Delhi - 110008 Ensure delivery by 27 July 2026. ESIC will not be responsible for postal delays.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required with Application
content_sections.text: Every applicant must submit self-attested copies of the following documents. Incomplete applications are liable to be summarily rejected without communication. Demand Draft / Banker's Cheque (original, pinned at top) Passport-size photograph (recent, firmly pasted) Date of Birth proof - Matriculation/10th certificate or mark sheet (School Leaving Certificate accepted for Tamil Nadu and Kerala candidates only) Degree certificates and mark sheets for all academic years (or provisional certificate with mark sheets) Experience certificates from each employer, clearly stating duration, nature of work, and basic/consolidated pay Research publication certificates/testimonials Caste/Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS) in the prescribed central government format OBC Non-Creamy Layer declaration (Annexure-II) Certificate of Disability (PwBD candidates) in the prescribed format - minimum 40% disability Ex-Servicemen certificate in prescribed proforma (if applicable) Declaration that current employer has been informed (for serving government/ESIC employees) Name-change supporting documents (if applicable - marriage certificate, divorce decree, or Deed Poll/Affidavit as appropriate)

content_sections.heading: ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 - Posting Locations
content_sections.text: Selected candidates are liable to be posted anywhere in India. ESIC operates medical colleges and PGIMSRs across the following states, and applicants must indicate at least 10 state preferences in the application form: Rajasthan, Bihar, Karnataka, West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Haryana, Telangana, Delhi, Maharashtra, Assam, Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, Jharkhand, and Gujarat. Preference will be considered subject to administrative requirements and cannot be claimed as a matter of right.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid
content_sections.text: These pitfalls are frequently responsible for application rejections - check each before dispatch: Not attaching the DD/BC - Applications without the prescribed fee from paying categories are summarily rejected with no appeal. Submitting multiple applications under different categories - All such applications will be rejected outright. Requesting to add experience after submission - Only experience with certificates submitted with the original application is considered for shortlisting. Using the wrong DD format - Online transfers, UPI, or cheques other than DD/Banker's Cheque are not accepted. Not superscribing the envelope - Failure to write the prescribed text on the cover may result in the application being overlooked. Unsigned application form - Unsigned or incomplete forms are liable to rejection without notice. Sending experience certificates without duration/pay details - Certificates must clearly state start date, end date, nature of duties, and pay. Vague letters will not be counted. Listing fewer than 10 state preferences - The notification specifically advises candidates to fill at least 10 options. Tampered or altered documents - Any forgery or alteration will lead to cancellation and potential legal action. Relying on postal speed alone - Send applications well in advance; postal delays are the applicant's responsibility.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply is 27 July 2026 for candidates in India. For candidates residing in Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Mizoram, Manipur, Nagaland, Tripura, Sikkim, Ladakh, Lahaul & Spiti, Andaman & Nicobar Islands, and Lakshadweep, the last date is 3 August 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 118 Professor vacancies are available across 22 medical specialties in ESIC PGIMSRs and ESIC Medical Colleges. The highest number of vacancies is in Transfusion Medicine (9), followed by General Medicine, General Surgery, and Orthopedics (8 each).

faq.question: Q3. Is there an online application for ESIC Professor 2026?
faq.answer: No. The application process is entirely offline. Candidates must fill out the prescribed application form and send it by Speed Post to the ESIC Regional Office in Delhi. Online applications are not accepted.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is ₹500 for General, OBC, EWS, and male ESIC/Government employees. SC, ST, PwBD candidates, and women of all categories are exempt from paying the fee. Payment must be made via Demand Draft or Banker's Cheque in favour of 'ESI Fund Account No. 2', payable at Delhi.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for ESIC Professor 2026?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit is 50 years as on 27 July 2026 for UR/EWS candidates. Age relaxation of 5 years applies to SC/ST, 3 years to OBC, 10 years to PwBD, and 5 years to ESIC/Government employees and Ex-Servicemen.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary for ESIC Professor 2026?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will be placed in Pay Level-13 of the Central Pay Matrix, with a basic pay range of ₹1,23,100 to ₹2,15,900 per month, plus Non-Practising Allowance, DA, HRA, and Transport Allowance as per applicable rules.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is based on an interview conducted by ESIC's Selection Board. There is no written examination. If applications are numerous, ESIC will shortlist candidates based on teaching experience beyond the minimum eligibility threshold before calling them for interview.

faq.question: Q8. What teaching experience is required to apply for ESIC Professor?
faq.answer: Candidates need either (a) 4 years of experience as Reader/Associate Professor in a recognized medical college, or (b) 3 years as Associate Professor with at least 4 indexed research publications as First Author, of which at least 2 must be from the tenure of Associate Professor. Experience as a General Duty Medical Officer does not qualify.

faq.question: Q9. Where will selected candidates be posted?
faq.answer: Selected candidates are liable to be posted anywhere in India across states where ESIC medical colleges and PGIMSRs are located. While the application requires at least 10 state preferences, postings are subject to administrative requirements and cannot be claimed as a right.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply is 27 July 2026 for candidates in India. For candidates residing in Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Mizoram, Manipur, Nagaland, Tripura, Sikkim, Ladakh, Lahaul & Spiti, Andaman & Nicobar Islands, and Lakshadweep, the last date is 3 August 2026.
